title: pinus nigra subsp. mauretanica
intro: Pinus nigra subsp. mauretanica, also known as the Moroccan black pine or Atlas pine, is a subspecies of evergreen coniferous tree native to the mountainous regions of North Africa. The essential oil extracted from the needles and bark of this plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Pinus nigra subsp. mauretanica include reducing stress and anxiety, improving respiratory health, and providing antioxidant protection against cell damage. This plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as essential oils, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
